steps.assistant_text: Alloc-cascade constructor. Body was already byte-exact (ret declared first -> IDO homes it at local slot 0x24 matching target's spill layout). The sole blocker was the target's single trailing 8-byte-alignment nop at 0xEF1C, needed so func_0000EF20 sits at 0xEF20. Landed by unwrapping the INCLUDE_ASM to the real C body and appending the one all-zero (nop) alignment word via SUFFIX_BYTES_FORCE := func_0000EE8C=0x00000000 -- the same legitimate alignment-pad mechanism already used for gui_func_0000161C and h2hproc fns. The prior 'cannot land' note wrongly assumed a GLOBAL_ASM dummy (8-byte -O2 floor); SUFFIX_BYTES_FORCE appends exactly one word, works mid-segment, ROM stays byte-identical.

metadata.verification: Body byte-exact vs target asm; trailing alignment nop via SUFFIX_BYTES_FORCE all-zero word. Full make => 'ROM OK (byte-identical to baserom)', cmp clean, verify-blocks OK. func_0000EF20 confirmed at 0xEF20.
